results.name: mana egg
results.description: Mana rocks that have to be sacrificed to add mana. They may ramp or merely filter your mana.

results.name: mana filter
results.description: Convert mana to other colors.

results.name: mana fix
results.description: Allows your lands to tap for additional color(s) of mana. See also [mana filter](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/mana-filter), [land conversion](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/land-conversion).

results.name: mana gorger
results.description: Cards that grow bigger when players cast spells.

results.name: mana increaser
results.description: Cards that increase the mana produced by mana producers, usually lands.

results.name: mana leak series
results.description: Soft counterspells with mana value N that cost more than N to pay off

results.name: manaless land
results.description: The vast majority of lands in the game can either tap for mana or be exchanged for another land that does. This tag represents the rare exceptions to that rule.

results.name: manaless value
results.description: Cards that can provide some amount of value without untapped lands.

results.name: mana producer
results.description: non-land cards which generate mana

results.name: mana restriction
results.description: Spells with unusual restrictions on how they can be paid for.

results.name: mana rig
results.description: Thran-built powerstone factories.

results.name: mana rock
results.description: Noncreature artifacts that net increase your mana.

results.name: mana sink
results.description: Cards with repeatable effects that you can dump a bunch of mana (at least 3) into each turn.

results.name: mana source (type)
results.description: These cards had the deprecated mana source type during some of their prints.

results.name: mana spent matters
results.description: Effects that care about the amount, type, and/or qualities of mana spent to do a thing.

results.name: mana storage
results.description: Cards that can be used to store mana for later phases and/or turns. The storing methods are varied—many use counters to count stored mana, while others create tokens.

results.name: mana symbol
results.description: Cards depicting one or more mana symbols explicitly

results.name: manifest ball
results.description: A visual element representing a manifested spell in Tarkir, seen as a blue energy ball.

results.name: man-o'-war
results.description: Creatures that can bounce your opponent's stuff when they enter the battlefield.

results.name: mantle
results.description: An item of clothing worn on and across both shoulders, accentuating them.

results.name: many legs
results.description: Creatures with 5+ legs. You know, more than the usual amount.

results.name: marauder
results.description: Enters and causes all players to sacrifice a creature.

results.name: marbling
results.description: A swirly effect made by suspending oil on water, creating an appearance similar to marble.

results.name: marchesa d'amati
results.description: Marchesa d'Amati, currently the Queen of Paliano.

results.name: mardu symbol
results.description: The dragon's wings.

results.name: marinus
results.description: Setting of the classic Doctor Who serial "The Keys of Marinus"

results.name: markov
results.description: A bloodline of vampires on Innistrad.

results.name: maro
results.description: Cards with P/T equal to cards in hand. Named after the card Maro, a nickname for Mark Rosewater, the head designer for Magic: The Gathering.

results.name: maro-sorcerer
results.description: Creatures that scale in power and toughness with the number of lands you control.

results.name: martyr
results.description: Creatures that sacrifice themselves for benefit.
